title: Allow editing already-uploaded Analysis video titles to be blank/empty

requirements.text: Update the backend `updateVideoTitle` (or equivalent) function so it accepts an empty or whitespace-only string as a valid title for an already-uploaded video. If the provided title is empty or whitespace, the backend should store a server-generated default title (e.g., based on upload date/time) rather than rejecting the request or trapping.

quotes: necesito que el titulo de los videos de la seccion analisis que ya estan subidos, tambien tengan la posibilidad de que su titulo sea opcional

acceptanceCriteria: Calling the update-title backend function with an empty string does not return an error or trap.
If the submitted title is empty or whitespace, the stored title is replaced with a non-empty auto-generated default (e.g., 'Video – <date>').
If a non-empty title is provided, it is stored as-is (existing behaviour preserved).

requirements.text: In the admin Video Management Panel (`VideoManagementPanel.tsx`), update the inline title-editing UI so the title field is optional. Remove any client-side validation that prevents saving an empty or whitespace title. The helper text next to the title input must indicate in English that the title is optional and that a default title will be generated automatically if left blank.

quotes: necesito que el titulo de los videos de la seccion analisis que ya estan subidos, tambien tengan la posibilidad de que su titulo sea opcional

acceptanceCriteria: The title input field in the video management panel does not show a validation error when left empty.
Submitting an empty title does not block the save action.
A helper label near the title field reads something like: 'Optional – leave blank to auto-generate a title.'
After saving an empty title, the video row reflects the auto-generated title returned from the backend.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture).
nonGoals: Changing the upload flow for new videos (already handled by existing features).
Modifying the public video gallery display logic.
